title: Talisman - Fifth Edition - A Casual Conversation
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9O3XYd0F6VI

A comprehensive comparison of Talisman 5th Edition (Avalon Hill) with 2nd and 3rd Editions, examining what's changed and whether it's worth getting.

**Who Should Get 5th Edition:**
- If you dislike Talisman: 5th Edition won't change your mind
- If you own an older version: The **Alliance expansion** (October 2024) adds official co-op/solo rules with light legacy elements - that's the draw
- If you're new: This video provides comparison info

**Major Rule Changes (marked in rulebook):**
- **Endgame**: Defeat a dragon boss (Str/Craft 10) instead of killing other players - more like 3rd Edition
- **Character selection**: Choose your character; last to choose goes first
- **Alignment**: Can change at game start
- **Carrying limit**: 4 objects + Talisman (Talismans don't count)
- **Death penalty**: Keep ALL items, gold, followers, spells - just respawn at Village
- **Fate tokens**: Can spend to move up to 6 spaces (huge!)
- **Toad curse**: Only lasts 1 turn, keep all cards
- **No "lose a turn"** spaces!
- **New space**: Caverns - random teleport, can reach Warlock's Cave turn 1
- **Tavern**: Pay 2 gold to ferry to Temple (middle region)

**Production Quality:**
- Smaller box than 4th Edition (similar to Elder Sign size)
- Stitched rulebook (like new HeroQuest)
- Full-size cards (no longer fit on board spaces)
- Plastic coins
- Quality standee cones return

**Board Comparison:**
- 5th Edition has fisheye perspective focused on Crown of Command
- River now properly on ground (not "sky river")
- Inner region spaces retained (unlike 3rd Edition's removal)

**Characters (5th Edition):**
Prophetess, Assassin, Ghoul, Sorceress, Wizard, Elf, Monk, Priest, Dwarf, Thief, Troll, Warrior

**Daniel's House Rules (continuing in 5th):**
- 7 trophies = any stat upgrade
- Cards for movement/combat
- Close enough rule
- Exploding 6s in combat

Excited for the Alliance expansion's co-op/solo/legacy direction!
